name: ui-fixture-inventory
question: What fixture/smoke-test infrastructure does this project have, and what conventions are in use?

schema.patterns: array of strings (the fixture/smoke-spec glob patterns detected in this project)

counts.fixtures: integer (count of files matching fixture patterns)
counts.smoke_specs: integer (count of files matching paired smoke-spec patterns)
counts.e2e_specs: integer (count of files matching e2e-spec patterns)

schema.conventions: array of strings (high-level conventions detected: 'co-located smoke', 'playwright', 'cypress', 'vitest fixtures', 'jest fixtures', 'pytest fixtures', etc.)
schema.briefing: string (≤200 chars; what the session-start hook emits as [fixture-inventory]; empty string suppresses the line)

consultation.note: Marked 'every' so the session-start hook iterator picks it up. Per-invocation cost is bounded by compute_cost_ms (200ms) and the oracle short-circuits on no-surface projects, so 'every' here means 'every session with a UI surface' in practice. Healthy fixtured projects emit empty briefing → line suppressed.

fallback: Manually inspect tests/ and e2e/ directories for fixture patterns. Common patterns: tests/fixtures/*-smoke.{ts,js,py} paired with tests/smoke/*.spec.{ts,js,py}; playwright.config.* presence; cypress/ directory. Detection rules documented in README.md and spec TGFP-02 acceptance criterion.

provenance.note: Universal Starter Set member. Composes with ui-surface-detector (ARIA-02) for the 'is there UI?' upstream question; this oracle answers the 'is there fixture infrastructure?' downstream question. Consumed by /0-uldf-ldis-plan Phase 4 testability gate Q4 (scaffolding-leverage scoring). Filesystem stat + small file reads only — never executes project scripts.
